agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a 2-hour window, premium pricing, and a refund policy for missed windows. Cross-functional stakeholders (PM, Ops, Finance, CS, Legal) have provided raw requirements with significant contradictions that must be resolved before implementation.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a specialized fulfillment mode within the broader Order/Delivery bounded context, touching Pricing, Refunds, Driver Assignment, and Real-Time Tracking subdomains.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery mode with a target delivery window of 2 hours from order placement, subject to eligibility constraints (distance, driver availability)." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A surcharge of $12.99 applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects Express Delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of delivery mode. Not subject to express refund policy." },
      { "term": "Express Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period starting from order placement within which delivery should be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute window from order placement within which a driver must physically pick up the express order from the warehouse." },
      { "term": "Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute window within which a replacement driver must be assigned after a mid-delivery driver cancellation." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Customer", "definition": "A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse that has the ordered items available." },
      { "term": "Express-Qualified Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is eligible to fulfill express orders." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A refund of the Express Fee issued when the Express Window is missed, characterized by Legal as a discretionary goodwill gesture rather than a contractual obligation." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exception", "definition": "A condition where the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, which voids eligibility for a Goodwill Refund." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express Delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — a 2-hour window is impossible to meet beyond this radius given logistics realities. Offering express outside this range would be systematically dishonest.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer address >10km from any warehouse, the Express Delivery option MUST NOT be selectable."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express orders.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational integrity — assigning lower-rated drivers to the premium tier undermines the service quality that justifies the express fee and erodes customer trust.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with rating < 4.0, that driver MUST NOT be assigned to any express order."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Express orders must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Ops has identified this as a hard prerequisite for meeting the 2-hour window. If pickup exceeds 15 minutes, the downstream delivery promise becomes physically infeasible.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order not picked up within 15 minutes, the system MUST escalate (exact escalation path TBD — see escalations)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The Express Fee is $12.99.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by Finance or Product. The amount is not a physical or logical constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer selecting Express Delivery, the Express Fee charged MUST equal the configured express fee amount ($12.99 at launch)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the Express Window is missed AND the customer did not provide a wrong address, the Express Fee (not the base delivery fee) is refunded.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Legal has characterized this as a goodwill gesture, not a contractual obligation. Policy could be modified or revoked. However, once granted, the refund scope (express fee only, not base fee) is a firm policy boundary.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed express window where customer address was valid, a refund of exactly the Express Fee amount MUST be issued. The base delivery fee MUST NOT be refunded."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No refund is issued when the Express Window is missed due to a wrong customer address.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Business policy to prevent abuse. Could be overridden by CS on a case-by-case basis.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed express window where the delivery address was flagged as incorrect, the system MUST NOT auto-issue a Goodwill Refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order and no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes, the Express Fee is refunded automatically.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Customer Success policy to preserve trust. The 10-minute threshold and auto-refund behavior are business decisions that could be adjusted.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation on an express order AND 10 minutes elapse with no replacement assigned, the system MUST automatically trigger a Goodwill Refund of the Express Fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express customers must have access to real-time driver tracking.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Product feature, not a domain invariant. However, it is a core value proposition of the express tier.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order in transit, the customer MUST be able to see the driver's real-time location."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse", "expected_behavior": "AMBIGUOUS — need to clarify whether 10km is inclusive (<=10km) or exclusive (<10km). Recommend inclusive." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup completes at 14:59 but delivery still misses the 2-hour window", "expected_behavior": "Refund policy still applies — the pickup SLA is an internal operational target, not a customer-facing exclusion. The customer sees only whether delivery arrived within 2 hours." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 118 of a 120-minute window", "expected_behavior": "Replacement window of 10 minutes will certainly exceed the express window. System should immediately trigger both a replacement attempt AND a preemptive refund since the window is mathematically unrecoverable." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address mid-delivery to one >10km from warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does this void the express guarantee? Does the order revert to standard? Needs stakeholder decision." },
    { "scenario": "No express-qualified drivers available at order time", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — should the express option be hidden, or shown as 'unavailable'? What if a driver becomes available 5 minutes later?" },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 mid-delivery (e.g., due to a concurrent review)", "expected_behavior": "Should NOT cancel an in-progress express delivery. Rating check applies at assignment time, not continuously." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouse proximity — customer is within 10km of two warehouses", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — which warehouse is selected? Closest? Most available inventory? This affects pickup SLA feasibility." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides a valid but hard-to-find address (apartment complex, no buzzer code)", "expected_behavior": "AMBIGUOUS — this is not a 'wrong address' per Finance's exclusion, but could cause a missed window. Refund eligibility unclear." },
    { "scenario": "Refund processing takes longer than 3 business days", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — no SLA breach consequence defined. Finance says 3 days but no escalation path if missed." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels, replacement assigned at minute 9, but replacement also cancels", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does the 10-minute replacement window restart? Or is the refund already locked in from the first cancellation chain?" }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express Fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream per eligible order. Refund liability is capped at the express fee only (not base fee), limiting downside. However, if missed-window rates are high, refund volume could erode margins significantly. The 'goodwill' framing gives flexibility to adjust policy without legal exposure.",
    "operational": "The 15-minute pickup SLA and 10-minute replacement window create hard operational pressure on dispatch and driver allocation. Express orders will likely require dedicated driver pools or priority queue logic. Real-time tracking adds infrastructure cost. Warehouse proximity checks require geocoding at order time.",
    "user": "Premium customers get faster delivery and real-time visibility, increasing satisfaction and willingness to pay. However, the Legal vs PM contradiction on 'guarantee' language creates a trust risk — if marketing says 'guaranteed' but terms say 'best effort,' customer backlash on missed windows will be amplified.",
    "risk": "PRIMARY RISK: The guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is a reputational and legal liability. If the UI says 'guaranteed 2-hour delivery' but legal terms disclaim it, customers will feel deceived. SECONDARY RISK: The wrong-address exclusion is subjective and dispute-prone without clear criteria for what constitutes a 'wrong' address.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Gain a premium delivery option with faster service and real-time tracking. Risk of confusion if guarantee language is inconsistent across touchpoints." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Higher-rated drivers get access to express orders (potential earnings increase). Tighter SLAs create performance pressure. Sub-4.0 drivers are excluded from a revenue tier." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must prioritize express order picking to meet the 15-minute pickup window. May require process changes or dedicated express picking lanes." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address exclusions, missed windows, and guarantee vs best-effort confusion. Need clear playbooks." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue line item plus refund processing overhead. Must build reporting for express fee revenue vs refund volume." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must ensure all customer-facing language avoids contractual guarantee framing. Terms of service update required before launch." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "The spec contains a fundamental contradiction between PM ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort, not a guarantee') that affects the entire product's identity, customer-facing language, and refund policy framing. Additionally, there are multiple undefined behaviors (address changes, driver availability, escalation paths) and an ambiguity on refund automation vs manual processing. Acceptance criteria cannot be finalized until the guarantee contradiction is resolved.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express Fee amount ($12.99) is clearly stated",
      "10km eligibility radius is agreed upon",
      "4-star minimum driver rating is clear",
      "Base delivery fee is excluded from refunds — all parties agree",
      "Wrong address voids refund eligibility"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — this is a direct contradiction",
      "Whether refunds are automatic or manual (CS says auto, Finance implies manual with 3-day processing)",
      "What constitutes a 'wrong address' for refund exclusion purposes",
      "Escalation path when pickup SLA is breached",
      "Whether express availability depends on real-time driver supply"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is measured as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not driving distance — needs confirmation",
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time",
      "Driver rating is the aggregate lifetime rating, not a rolling window",
      "A single warehouse must be within range — the system does not combine multiple warehouses"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM states the 2-hour delivery is 'guaranteed' while Legal explicitly says it is 'best effort' and 'not a contractual guarantee.' This is not a nuance — it is a direct contradiction that affects product naming, UI copy, marketing materials, refund policy framing, and legal liability. The word 'guarantee' cannot appear in customer-facing materials if Legal's position holds, yet the PM's product vision is built around it.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Legal", "Marketing", "Customers", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Adopt Legal's position — frame as 'Express Delivery with a 2-hour target' and refund as 'goodwill.' Safer but weaker value proposition.",
        "B: Adopt PM's position — make it a contractual guarantee with Legal drafting enforceable terms. Stronger value prop but higher liability.",
        "C: Hybrid — use 'guarantee' in marketing with asterisk/fine print disclaiming contractual commitment. Common in industry but creates trust risk."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A is safest for launch. Use language like '2-hour express delivery' without the word 'guarantee.' Frame the refund as a proactive customer care policy. Revisit Option B once operational data shows missed-window rates are acceptably low (e.g., <5%)."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Refund processing is described inconsistently. Customer Success says replacement failure triggers an 'automatic' refund. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These could be compatible (auto-triggered but takes 3 days to process) or contradictory (auto means instant credit vs manual batch processing). The refund trigger mechanism and timeline must be explicitly agreed upon.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance", "Customer Success", "Engineering", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-trigger refund in system, but actual funds transfer follows Finance's 3-business-day processing SLA.",
        "B: Instant credit to customer wallet/account, with Finance reconciling in batch.",
        "C: Manual CS review before any refund is issued, processed within 3 days."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — the system automatically creates a refund record when conditions are met (missed window, driver cancellation timeout), but the actual payment reversal follows standard Finance processing timelines. This satisfies both CS's automation requirement and Finance's processing reality."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "No definition of 'wrong address' exists. Finance says no refund for wrong addresses, but there is no criteria for what qualifies. Is it a typo in the street number? A valid address where the customer no longer lives? An address that exists but the driver can't find? Without clear criteria, this becomes a subjective CS judgment call and a source of customer disputes.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance", "Customer Support", "Customers", "Legal"],
      "options": [
        "A: Define 'wrong address' as an address that does not exist or cannot be geocoded to a valid location.",
        "B: Include addresses where the customer is not present or refuses delivery.",
        "C: Leave to CS discretion with guidelines."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A for automated exclusion (non-geocodable or non-existent addresses). All other address-related issues should be CS discretion with documented guidelines, not automated refund denial."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No escalation path is defined when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached. Ops says the 2-hour window can't be met if pickup takes >15 minutes, but there's no defined action: cancel the express promise? Notify the customer? Auto-refund? Attempt delivery anyway? This is a critical operational gap.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations", "Customers", "Customer Support", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-notify customer that express window may be missed, offer option to cancel for full refund.",
        "B: Continue delivery attempt, pre-authorize the goodwill refund if window is ultimately missed.",
        "C: Reassign to a different driver immediately."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — continue the delivery (the order is already placed and customer is waiting). Pre-flag the order as 'at risk' internally. If the window is ultimately missed, the refund triggers normally. Do not burden the customer with operational details."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No guidance on express order availability when no express-qualified drivers are available. Should the express option disappear from the UI? Show as greyed out? Should the system check real-time driver supply before offering express?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Operations", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Only show express option when at least one qualified driver is available within the zone.",
        "B: Always show express if customer is within 10km; handle driver unavailability after order placement.",
        "C: Show express with a warning about potential delays when driver supply is low."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — do not offer a service you cannot fulfill. Check real-time driver availability as part of express eligibility. This prevents orders that will immediately breach the pickup SLA."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot proceed to implementation with 'guarantee' language until PM and Legal align on whether this is a contractual guarantee or best-effort SLA.", "business_rule_broken": "Fundamental product identity is contradicted between stakeholders." },
    { "violation": "Cannot define refund acceptance criteria until the auto vs manual refund process is clarified between CS and Finance.", "business_rule_broken": "BR-5 and BR-7 refund triggers are ambiguous in execution." }
  ]
}
